description:
You are acting as a Linux systems-engineer who has just finished tightening several system
    configuration files.  
    Your manager now wants you to do three things:

    1. Collect the hardened configuration files that are already present in  
       /home/user/hardening/   (they have just been staged for you).  
       The directory currently contains exactly three plain-text files:  
           • sysctl_hardened.conf  
           • sshd_hardened.conf  
           • pam_hardened.conf  

    2. Create a compressed archive named hardening_backup_2024Q2.tar.gz that contains ONLY
       the three files listed above (no directories, no extra metadata).  
       Store this archive at /home/user/archives/  (create the directory if it does not yet exist).

    3. Immediately test the archive by extracting it into  
       /home/user/recovery_test/   and making sure the three files appear there byte-for-byte.

    Finally, you must create a plain-text operation log file at  
       /home/user/operation_logs/backup_audit.log   (create the directory if needed).  
       The log MUST contain exactly four lines, each line recording one of the following actions
       in the order they occurred:

         1) directory_created             – when/if you create /home/user/archives  
         2) archive_created               – after the .tar.gz archive is finished  
         3) extraction_test_directory     – when/if you create /home/user/recovery_test  
         4) archive_extracted_success     – after verifying that all three files are present

       Log line format (very strict – the grader will parse it verbatim):

         [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] ACTION

       • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS must be the current system date & time in 24-hour notation with
         leading zeros where necessary.  
       • ACTION must be one of the four literal tokens shown above.  
       • There is exactly one single space after the closing bracket.  
       • No extra spaces or characters are allowed.  
       • Each log entry must be on its own line and terminated by a Unix newline (LF).

    When everything is done:

      • /home/user/archives/hardening_backup_2024Q2.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed
        tar archive containing exactly three members:  
            sysctl_hardened.conf, sshd_hardened.conf, pam_hardened.conf

      • /home/user/recovery_test/ holds the three files extracted from the archive and they must be
        byte-identical to their originals in /home/user/hardening/.

      • /home/user/operation_logs/backup_audit.log exists, is world-readable, and matches the
        strict four-line format specified above.

    The entire workflow can be accomplished with roughly 5-10 standard GNU/Linux commands
    (mkdir, tar, cp/mv, ls, diff, etc.).  
    Do NOT delete the original /home/user/hardening/ directory or its files.

    The automated grading script will:

      1. Check the presence and validity of the tar.gz archive.  
      2. Extract the archive in a clean directory and compare file checksums.  
      3. Parse backup_audit.log to ensure it contains exactly the four required lines in the
         specified syntax and chronological order.

    Make sure you run all commands from a regular user shell; no root privileges are available.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the grader. Do NOT reveal in the public task text.
    -->
    <pre_existing_files>
        /home/user/hardening/sysctl_hardened.conf
            # Hardened sysctl settings
            net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0
            net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0

        /home/user/hardening/sshd_hardened.conf
            # Hardened SSH daemon configuration
            PermitRootLogin no
            PasswordAuthentication no

        /home/user/hardening/pam_hardened.conf
            # Hardened PAM configuration
            password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3
            auth required pam_faillock.so preauth audit silent deny=5 unlock_time=900
    </pre_existing_files>

    <expected_archive>
        path: /home/user/archives/hardening_backup_2024Q2.tar.gz
        members:
            - sysctl_hardened.conf
            - sshd_hardened.conf
            - pam_hardened.conf
    </expected_archive>

    <extraction_destination>
        /home/user/recovery_test/
        # All three files above must be present here with identical SHA256 sums
    </extraction_destination>

    <log_file>
        path: /home/user/operation_logs/backup_audit.log
        # Regex that the grader will use (four lines, strict ordering):
        # ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] directory_created\n
        # ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] archive_created\n
        # ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] extraction_test_directory\n
        # ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] archive_extracted_success\n
    </log_file>

    <permissions>
        # Both /home/user/archives and /home/user/operation_logs must end up with 755
        # Archive file and log file must be 644
    </permissions>
name: task_000000_c8bc3afc
